next_steps: Perform BRAF V600E mutation testing on the tibial biopsy specimen
Complete immunohistochemical staining for CD1a, langerin, CD68, and CD163 to characterize the infiltrate
Order serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and serum free light chain assay
Coordinate systemic therapy evaluation, considering interferon-alpha or BRAF/MEK inhibitors if BRAF positive
Initiate referral to a multidisciplinary center specialized in histiocytic disorders
Perform BRAF V600E mutation analysis on the tibial biopsy specimen
Perform immunohistochemical staining for CD1a, langerin, CD68, and CD163 to distinguish histiocytic disorders
Order serum IgG4 levels and serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation
Consult a hematopathologist for expert review of all previous bone and retroperitoneal biopsy slides
Obtain serum markers of systemic inflammation (ESR, CRP)
Refer for specialized multidisciplinary care in histiocytic or multisystem inflammatory disorders
Initiate evaluation for targeted therapy pending BRAF/MAPK mutation status
